articles.title: Why the Moon Changes Shape
articles.summary: The Moon looks different as sunlight hits it from different angles.
articles.body: The Moon does not make its own light. We see sunlight bouncing from its surface. As the Moon moves around Earth, we see different parts of the lit side.
articles.tryThis: Draw the Moon three nights in a row and compare the shape.

articles.title: Rainforests Are Layered Homes
articles.summary: Rainforests have layers where different plants and animals live.
articles.body: A rainforest has a forest floor, understory, canopy, and emergent layer. Each layer has different light, food, and shelter.
articles.tryThis: Build a four-layer forest from paper and label each layer.

articles.title: Your Brain Gets Better With Practice
articles.summary: Practice helps the brain make stronger learning paths.
articles.body: When you practice reading, drawing, building, moving, or solving, your brain gets better at sending useful signals.
articles.tryThis: Pick one small skill and practice it for five minutes.

articles.title: How Bridges Hold Weight
articles.summary: Bridges use shape and material to spread weight.
articles.body: A bridge works by spreading weight into supports. Triangles, arches, beams, and cables can all help a bridge stay strong.
articles.tryThis: Make a paper bridge and test how many coins it can hold.
